revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Iwabaru Kofun cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Iwabaru Kofun cluster | native_name =岩原古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Iwabaru Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Iwabaru Kofun | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Iwabaru Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|59|46|N|130|40|11|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|Yamaga, Kumamoto|qid=Q861214|lt=Yamaga, Kumamoto|1=Yamaga, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =c. 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No facilities | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|59|46|N|130|40|11|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Iwabaru Kofun cluster'''|岩原古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Kao-cho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|Yamaga, Kumamoto|qid=Q861214|lt= Yamaga|1=Yamaga, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}} in [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus group was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1958, with the area under protection subsequently extended in 1999. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206713|title= 岩原古墳群|trans-title= Iwabaru Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=October 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Iwabaru Kofun cluster is located on the Iwabaru Plateau, about 75 meters above sea level, on the left bank of the middlereaches of the Kikuchi River. This group of tumuli dates from the middleto late Kofun period and consists of the {{nihongo|Futagozuka Kofun|双子塚古墳|}} and eight {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tombs with diameters of about 20 to 30 meters. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Futagozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, orientated almost northwest. It has a main axis of about 107 meters long, a diameter of the circular rear part of about 57 meters, a width of the front rectangular portion of about 50 meters, and a height of about 9 meters. It is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' all over, is built in three tiers, and has traces of a surrounding moat. It is also called the "Iwabaru Kofun".<ref name="Shiseki"/>
An additional tumulus was designated in 1965, when a house-shaped Sarcophagus was exposed during construction of a farm road. It is located about 150 meters south of Futagozuka Kofun and is estimated to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of a few dozen meters. The northern half of the mound was lost due to this work, and only the southern half remains today. Seven more circular tumuli have been confirmed since then. The [[Kumamoto Prefectural Decorated Kofun Museum]] is located in one corner of the group of tombs.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|qid=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1852 Cultural Heritage of Miyazaki prefecture] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://kofunkan.pref.kumamoto.jp/iwabaru/ Kumamoto Precture Decorated Tomb Museum] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
